title: Podman API service failed
body:
### Issue Description

The podman API service fails to start with a systemd service file or running the podman command.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run podman service systemd start:
```
$ systemctl --user start podman.service
```
or run any command involving **podman** with log-level=info:
```
podman --log-level=debug info
```
or 
```
$ podman run -it --rm archlinux bash -c "echo hello world"
```



### Describe the results you received

```
systemctl --user status podman.service
× podman.service - Podman API Service
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.service; disabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Wed 2025-11-12 19:07:09 CET; 3h 23min ago
   Duration: 15ms
 Invocation: e69109f16f3b456fbf1c424333ff0197
TriggeredBy: ● podman.socket
       Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
    Process: 340663 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman $LOGGING system service (code=exited, status=125)
   Main PID: 340663 (code=exited, status=125)
   Mem peak: 19.6M
        CPU: 23ms

Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia systemd[6723]: Starting Podman API Service...
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia systemd[6723]: Started Podman API Service.
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia podman[340663]: time="2025-11-12T19:07:09+01:00" level=info msg="/usr/bin/podman filtering at log level info"
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia podman[340663]: time="2025-11-12T19:07:09+01:00" level=info msg="Setting parallel job count to 85"
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia podman[340663]: time="2025-11-12T19:07:09+01:00" level=info msg="Using sqlite as database backend"
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia podman[340663]: Error: default OCI runtime "kata-runtime" not found: invalid argument
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia systemd[6723]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Nov 12 19:07:09 magnolia systemd[6723]: podman.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
```
The socket is active and listening, but its service file didn't triggered the **podman.service** as it is supposed to do.

Now about **podman** command:
```
$ podman --log-level=debug info
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called info.PersistentPreRunE(podman --runtime /usr/bin/kata-runtime --log-level=debug info)
INFO[0000] Setting parallel job count to 85
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"
INFO[0000] Using sqlite as database backend
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/gabx/.local/share/containers/storage
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1000/containers
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/gabx/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/gabx/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
DEBU[0000] Using transient store: false
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that metacopy is not being used
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that native-diff is usable
DEBU[0000] backingFs=btrfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=true, usingMetacopy=false
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime crun-vm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-vm: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime crun-wasm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-wasm: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime krun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime krun: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime ocijail initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime ocijail: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runj initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runj: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime youki initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime youki: invalid argument
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/kata-runtime"
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that volatile is being used
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that idmapped mounts for overlay are not supported
DEBU[0000] Check for idmapped mounts support
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf"
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/00-shortnames.conf"
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/10-unqualified-serach-registries.conf"
```
Now running a container:
```
% podman run -it --rm archlinux bash -c "echo hello world"
Resolved "archlinux" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/00-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/archlinux:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 849b0775ea0f done   |
Copying blob a59b1e251b85 done   |
Copying config d60cf1d6ee done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
/etc/kata-containers/configuration.toml: file /usr/share/kata-containers/vmlinux.container does not exist
                                                                                                         ERRO[0005] Removing container 59adb957b0e0cf4edfeedb10824ecc7f8e33131b163b3165e5cc8f5316b81f86 from runtime after creation failed
Error: OCI runtime error: /usr/bin/kata-runtime: /etc/kata-containers/configuration.toml: file /usr/share/kata-containers/vmlinux.container does not exist
```


### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I can't find any `/usr/share/kata-containers/vmlinux.container` file: It looks like no arch linux package provide this kernel.
```
% ls -al /usr/share/kata-containers/

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Is the use of kata-containers as the default OCI runtime intentional? I believe they dropped support for their OCI-compliant runtime, and as such may no longer be compatible with Podman

My user _containers.conf_ file has **crun** defined as default OCI runtime. So why this kata-runtime story in my issue?
I removed from my system any package runtime related. Now, I have a new error:
```
podman run hello-world
Error: no valid executable found for OCI runtime /usr/bin/kata-runtime: invalid argument
```

Why is podman trying to call OCI runtime?

More info:
```
trollolo@magnolia ➤➤ ~/.config % podman --log-level debug run hello-world  >/tmp/debug.log 2>&1
trollolo@magnolia ➤➤ ~/.config % cat /tmp/debug.log
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Called run.PersistentPreRunE(podman --runtime /usr/bin/kata-runtime --log-level debug run hello-world)"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=info msg="Setting parallel job count to 85"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Using conmon: \"/usr/bin/conmon\""
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=info msg="Using sqlite as database backend"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Using graph driver overlay"

time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Using run root /run/user/60094/containers"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Using static dir /home/trollolo/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Using tmp dir /run/user/60094/libpod/tmp"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Using volume path /home/trollolo/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Using transient store: false"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="[graphdriver] trying provided driver \"overlay\""
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Cached value indicated that overlay is supported"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Cached value indicated that overlay is supported"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Cached value indicated that metacopy is not being used"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Cached value indicated that native-diff is usable"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="backingFs=btrfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=true, usingMetacopy=false"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Initializing event backend journald"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime crun-vm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-vm: invalid argument"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime crun-wasm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-wasm: invalid argument"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime youki initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime youki: invalid argument"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime krun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime krun: invalid argument"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime runj initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runj: invalid argument"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument"
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime ocijail initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime ocijail: invalid argument"
Error: no valid executable found for OCI runtime /usr/bin/kata-runtime: invalid argument
time="2025-11-17T22:49:23+01:00" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"
```

`podman --runtime /usr/bin/kata-runtime`

Your Podman binary is being called with the `--runtime` argument to force Kata, evidently? Either deliberately or through an alias.

F* hell!! Well done. No idea why, I had an alias in one my zsh startup files. Removing it solved this issue.
```
 podman run hello-world
!... Hello Podman World ...!

         .--"--.
       / -     - \
      / (O)   (O) \
   ~~~| -=(,Y,)=- |
    .---. /`  \   |~~
 ~/  o  o \~~~~.----. ~~
  | =(X)= |~  / (O (O) \
   ~~~~~~~  ~| =(Y_)=-  |
  ~~~~    ~~~|   U      |~~
